# Routing Rules

## Rule 1 — Do not use BCC by default

Use SCC first. Add BCC only when EXP-002 or segment evidence supports anchors.

## Rule 2 — Use BCC+ when visual context matters

Use BCC+ for crowded frames, muted-first contexts, demos, or platform-safe placement needs.

## Rule 3 — Use labels in action mode

During visual action, prefer minimal labels over sentence captions.

## Rule 4 — Use full captions for access

Never route to overlay-only access.

## Rule 5 — Segment results

A method can be accepted for one content type and rejected for another.

## Rule 6 — Negative controls must fail

If HBC or confusion overlays perform unexpectedly well, inspect metrics. The test may be rewarding confusion rather than comprehension.
